data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse represents an ideological legitimation crisis when ruler-god contractual theology failed against evidence of divine impotence (drought, plague, defeat), but Egyptian theology embedded pharaoh within cosmic cyclicity rather than conditional reciprocity, enabling narrative reabsorption of catastrophe", "mechanism": "Theological-institutional endurance: Mycenaean wanax and Hittite tabarna legitimacy required demonstrable divine favor through prosperity and victory; Egyptian ma'at ideology transformed disorder (isfet) into expected cosmic phase requiring pharaonic ritual restoration rather than regime delegitimation", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean wanax disappearance", "Hittite king's ritual functions", "Ugarit Baal/Yamm mythology political coding", "Egyptian 19th-20th Dynasty theological continuity"], "excludes": ["Material provisioning systems", "Military organization", "Trade network topology"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Egyptian 19th Dynasty shows explicit pharaonic apologetics and usurpation precisely during crisis decades (Ramesses III assassination, Twosret supplantation)", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Hittite vassal treaties and oracle texts show theological flexibility accepting temporary divine disfavor without regime collapse", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Mycenaean palatial collapse correlates with wanax title disappearance from Linear B but post-palatial Greece retains basileus with identical ritual functions", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Radical ideational monism contravening materialist consensus; deliberately atypical in causal priority"}
